mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL reflects accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL increased month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE greater than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals weakening labor demand that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS above 5% combined with month-over-month contraction in INDPRO indicates restrictive monetary policy converging with slowing industrial activity, which jointly compresses equity risk appetite and produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform FEDFUNDS alone in predicting negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO signals expanding industrial capacity utilization that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of post-recovery months.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in RSAFS month-over-month growth signals robust consumer spending that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of weeks when RSAFS accelerated month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS over a 3-month window confirms labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate margin expectations, jointly driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month and PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The combined UNRATE and PAYEMS signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of joint trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals monetary easing that lowers discount rates and credit costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the rate cut exceeds 25 basis points.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines exceed 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 alongside elevated FEDFUNDS above 4% indicates resilient output despite restrictive monetary policy, which supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds 0% and FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered quarters.
